title: CSS Selectors, Tree Navigation, Cardinality, Text, and Attributes
description: A brittle absolute selector returns the wrong repeated card, missing attributes become strings, nested text collapses, and a parser upgrade silently changes cardinality. Learners cumulatively produce selector and extraction contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use type, class, ID, attribute, combinator, grouping, and supported pseudo-class selectors with explicit scope and library support.
State zero, one, optional, at-least-one, and many cardinality expectations and reject ambiguous or duplicate matches.
Anchor extraction at a stable semantic component and resolve fields relative to it instead of coupling to page-wide positions.
